{"body":"## Summary
This PR adds a new term for 'uterine fundus' as requested in issue #3637.

## Term Details
- **ID**: UBERON:9900001
- **Name**: uterine fundus
- **Definition**: The superior, dome-shaped portion of the uterus
- **Synonyms**: 
  - fundus uteri (exact, latin)
  - fundus of uterus (exact)
- **Parent**: organ part (UBERON:0000064)
- **Relationships**: part_of uterus (UBERON:0000995)
- **Contributor**: Aleix Puig-Barbé (https://orcid.org/0000-0001-6677-8489)

## Important Note
The PMIDs provided in the original issue (PMID:41204538 and PMID:40653088) appear to be incorrect, as they exceed the current range of valid PMIDs. I've temporarily used PMID:39112955 from the synonym reference. 

**Action needed**: Please verify and provide the correct PMIDs for the definition before merging this PR.

## Changes Made
- Added new term UBERON:9900001 with all required metadata
- Included proper attribution and timestamps
- Linked to issue #3637 via term_tracker_item

Closes #3637

content.content:
# Research Article Summary

**Title:** "Collagenous gastritis: histopathologic features and association with other gastrointestinal diseases"

**Authors:** Stanley T Leung, Vishal S Chandan, Joseph A Murray, Tsung-Teh Wu (Mayo Clinic, 2009)

## Does it describe subepithelial collagen bands?

Yes, extensively. The abstract defines collagenous gastritis as a condition "characterized by the deposition of a subepithelial collagen band and accompanying inflammatory infiltrate." 

The study measured collagen thickness, reporting "the range of subepithelial collagen thickness was 15 to 120 microm in CG." Researchers also noted that "the collagenous layer showed surface epithelial injury and entrapped inflammatory cells."

The research examined 23 gastric biopsies to evaluate these pathological features and their associations with autoimmune and intestinal diseases, finding notably different patterns between pediatric and adult patient populations.

content.content:
# Title and Abstract Summary

**Title:** "Collagenous Gastritis Is an Underdiagnosed Cause of Anemia and Abdominal Pain: Systematic Scoping Review"

**Regarding Collagen Description:**

Yes, the abstract explicitly describes subepithelial collagen deposition. It states the condition is "characterized by infiltration of the lamina propria with mononuclear cells and subepithelial deposition of collagen."

The results section also confirms this finding: "65.9% of histopathologic findings included subepithelial collagen bands, and 37.5% had mucosal inflammatory infiltrates."

So the document directly addresses both collagenous bands and subepithelial collagen as key pathological features of this gastric condition.

## Summary

Adds new term **collagen band** (UBERON:1200005) as requested in #3727.

## Term details

- **ID**: UBERON:1200005
- **Name**: collagen band
- **Definition**: "A band-shaped collection of collagen fibrils forming a distinct layer beneath an epithelium." [PMID:19295410, PMID:37022603]
- **Parent**: UBERON:0011860 (collection of collagen fibrils)
- **Synonyms** (all EXACT):
  - subepithelial collagen band [PMID:19295410]
  - collagenous band [PMID:37022603]
  - subepithelial collagen layer
  - superficial subepithelial collagen layer
  - subepithelial collagenous band [PMID:19295410]
- **Contributor**: Aleix Puig-Barbé (https://orcid.org/0000-0001-6677-8489)

## ⚠ Note on PMIDs

The issue cited **PMID:25216277** as both definition source and synonym source for "subepithelial collagen layer" / "superficial subepithelial collagen layer". However that PMID resolves to a paper on transoral palate-sparing nasopharyngectomy with the Flex System, which is unrelated to subepithelial collagen layers. I therefore used **PMID:19295410** (Leung et al. 2009, *Collagenous gastritis: histopathologic features and association with other gastrointestinal diseases*) and **PMID:37022603** (a 2023 scoping review on collagenous gastritis) — both of which explicitly describe the "subepithelial collagen band" concept.

Synonyms whose only citation was PMID:25216277 have been left without an xref pending clarification. Please confirm the correct PMID in review.

Closes #3727
